description: Payroll engine configuration for ML Systems. Handles gross-up from Day N net pay, all federal/RI tax calculations, employer costs, compliance calendar, and deposit schedules. Designed to replicate Gusto-level payroll functionality.

brackets2026.note: 2025 brackets used as proxy for 2026. Verify when IRS publishes Rev. Proc. for 2026.

w4Default.note: Each employee submits W-4. Default assumes single/0 for maximum withholding safety.

riIncomeTax.note: RI uses flat brackets with standard deduction + personal exemption. Verify against RI Division of Taxation 2026 tables.

socialSecurity.note: Wage base increases annually. 2025 = $168,600. 2026 TBD — using 2025 as proxy.

medicare.note: No wage cap. Additional 0.9% employee-only above $200K.

riTdi.name: Rhode Island Temporary Disability Insurance
riTdi.note: Employee-only tax. Includes Job Development Fund (JDF). Deducted from wages. Wage base increases annually.

futa.name: Federal Unemployment Tax Act
futa.note: 0.6% effective after state credit. Only on first $7,000 of wages per employee.

riSuta.name: Rhode Island State Unemployment Insurance
riSuta.experienceRating: Rate adjusts after 3 years based on claim history. New employer rate = 2.42%.
riSuta.note: Verify wage base and new employer rate with RI DLT for 2026.

workersCompensation.basis: per $100 of payroll, varies by class code and experience mod
workersCompensation.note: Actual rate depends on carrier, class code assignment, experience mod. Deconstruction may require higher-risk class code.

grossUpCalculation.description: Since Day N net pay changes daily, standard annualized withholding creates over/under-withholding. The YTD cumulative method recalculates total tax liability through day N, subtracts prior withholdings, and the difference is today's withholding.
steps: 1. Determine desired net for Day N = $N
2. Estimate Day N gross using prior day's marginal rate as starting point
3. Add Day N gross estimate to YTD gross
4. Calculate total YTD federal tax on (YTD gross - standard deduction)
5. Calculate total YTD RI tax on (YTD gross - RI standard deduction - personal exemption)
6. Calculate YTD FICA (SS if under wage base + Medicare)
7. Calculate YTD TDI (if under wage base)
8. Total YTD employee deductions = sum of steps 4-7
9. YTD net = YTD gross - YTD total deductions
10. If YTD net ≠ target YTD net (sum 1..N), adjust Day N gross and repeat
11. Converge via Newton's method or binary search (2-3 iterations)

marginalRates.description: Combined marginal rate at different income levels determines gross-up factor

examples.note: SS wage base exceeded, Medicare only

year1.effectiveTaxBurden: Sal pays $37,298 in taxes/insurance per employee above the $66,795 net. That's 55.8% overhead on net pay.

dailyPayrollProcess.description: Step-by-step daily payroll processing — what happens every single day

steps.action: Determine Day N for each employee
steps.detail: Each employee may have a different start date → different Day N. Employee hired Jan 1 = Day N on calendar. Employee hired Mar 15 = Day 1 on Mar 15, Day 2 on Mar 16, etc.
Use YTD cumulative method to determine gross that yields $N net after all deductions.
If employee worked project hours today and Day N gross / hours worked < $15/hr, increase gross to $15/hr × hours. Recalculate net (will exceed $N). Track the supplement separately.
Federal income tax, RI income tax, Social Security, Medicare, RI TDI. Use YTD method for accuracy.
Day #, hours worked (if project day), gross, each deduction line, net, YTD totals for all categories.
ACH to employee bank account. Same-day or next business morning.
FICA match, FUTA, SUTA, WC — added to weekly/monthly accrual register.

weeklyAggregation.description: Every Sunday (or Monday morning), aggregate the week's payroll data
produces: Hours log for minimum wage compliance verification

daily: Process Day N payroll for each employee
Record gross, deductions, net in payroll ledger
weekly: Verify minimum wage compliance for any project days
monthly: Federal tax deposit by 15th of following month (Form 941 liability)
RI withholding tax deposit by 15th of following month
Reconcile payroll ledger to bank statements
quarterly: File Form 941 (federal quarterly employment tax return) — due April 30, July 31, Oct 31, Jan 31
File RI UI quarterly wage report + pay UI contributions
Review workers comp payroll audit data
annually: Issue W-2 to each employee by January 31
File W-3 transmittal with SSA by January 31
File RI-W3 with RI Division of Taxation
File Form 940 (annual FUTA return) by January 31
Review RI UI experience rating for rate adjustment (after year 3)
1-YEAR REVIEW GATE for each employee on their anniversary

federal.ein: Apply via IRS Form SS-4 (online at irs.gov) — required before first payroll
federal.form941: Quarterly employment tax return. Reports federal income tax withheld + employee/employer FICA.
federal.formW2: Annual wage/tax statement per employee. Due Jan 31 to employee + SSA.
federal.formW4: Employee fills out at hire. Determines federal withholding.

rhodeIsland.riWithholding: Register with RI Division of Taxation for withholding account
rhodeIsland.riUi: Register with RI DLT for UI account. Quarterly wage reports + contributions.
rhodeIsland.riTdi: Employee TDI deduction reported on quarterly UI filings
rhodeIsland.riNewHire: Report new hires within 14 days to RI New Hire Directory (riNewhire.com)

requirements: ACH origination capability (for daily employee deposits)
Separate payroll tax escrow account (recommended — holds withholdings until deposit dates)
Estimated monthly cash flow: see cashFlowModel

month1.note: Days 1–30 for 4 employees. Very light cash demand.

month12.note: Days 335–365. Peak Year 1 cash demand. ~$74K/month for 4 crew.

year1Total.note: Full Year 1 employer outflow for 4 crew.

achDetails.alternative: Batch daily amounts, deposit weekly. Workers see daily stubs but receive 1 weekly ACH. Reduces transactions 7×. Trade-off: workers wait up to 6 days for earliest daily amount in batch.
achDetails.recommendation: Daily deposits align with Day N philosophy — the worker sees $N hit their account every day. The cost ($365–$2,190/year) is worth the psychological impact.

needsExternalService: Actual ACH processing (bank or payment processor)
E-filing of 941/940/W-2 (can use IRS e-file or SSA BSO)
RI e-filing (RI Division of Taxation portal)

gustoComparison.whatGustoDoes: Runs payroll, files taxes, handles deposits, manages compliance, generates pay stubs, handles W-2s
gustoComparison.recommendation: For Year 1, Gusto or similar payroll provider ($768–$912/year) handles ALL tax filing, deposit, and compliance. The Day N model is unusual but Gusto supports custom pay rates. Set each employee's daily rate to their Day N amount. The payroll-engine.json gives you full visibility into what Gusto is doing so you're never blind to your own numbers.

integrationPoints.payStructure: ontology/pay-structure.json — Day N formula, review gate, role assignments
integrationPoints.financialArchitect: Pro forma integration — crew cost per home, per project, per year
integrationPoints.housing2030: Grant scoring — labor costs affect TDC, which affects scoring
integrationPoints.deconSessions: src/lib/schema.ts — link payroll to project sessions for cost-per-home tracking
integrationPoints.bohListings: The 45 contribution tracking — listings created during solve time tied to employee
